title: psychotria carnea subsp. oncocarpa
intro: Psychotria carnea subsp. oncocarpa, also known as the red-veined jungle mint, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Australia and New Guinea, characterized by its distinctive red veins and medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its rich content of flavonoids and phenolic compounds. The health benefits of Psychotria carnea subsp. oncocarpa include its potential to treat wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to act as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or infusion by steeping its leaves in hot water, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
